package main

import "testing"

func TestProcessUser(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name, email string
		age         int
		want        string
	}{
		{"Alice", "alice@example.com", 30, "User: Alice (age: 30, email: alice@example.com)"},
		{"", "alice@example.com", 30, "Error: name is required"},
		{"Alice", "alice@example.com", -1, "Error: age must be positive"},
		{"Alice", "", 30, "Error: email is required"},
		{"Alice", "ab", 30, "Error: email is too short"},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := ProcessUser(tt.name, tt.age, tt.email)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("ProcessUser(%q, %d, %q) = %q, want %q", tt.name, tt.age, tt.email, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestProcessProduct(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name, category string
		price          float64
		want           string
	}{
		{"Widget", "Electronics", 29.99, "Product: Widget (price: 29.99, category: Electronics)"},
		{"", "Electronics", 29.99, "Error: name is required"},
		{"Widget", "Electronics", -1, "Error: price must be positive"},
		{"Widget", "", 29.99, "Error: category is required"},
		{"Widget", "A", 29.99, "Error: category name is too short"},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := ProcessProduct(tt.name, tt.price, tt.category)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("ProcessProduct(%q, %f, %q) = %q, want %q", tt.name, tt.price, tt.category, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestProcessOrder(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		id, status string
		amount     float64
		want       string
	}{
		{"ORD-001", "pending", 99.99, "Order: ORD-001 (amount: 99.99, status: pending)"},
		{"", "pending", 99.99, "Error: id is required"},
		{"ORD-001", "pending", -1, "Error: amount must be positive"},
		{"ORD-001", "", 99.99, "Error: status is required"},
		{"ORD-001", "AB", 99.99, "Error: status is too short"},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := ProcessOrder(tt.id, tt.amount, tt.status)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("ProcessOrder(%q, %f, %q) = %q, want %q", tt.id, tt.amount, tt.status, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestProcessCustomer(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		name, email, phone string
		age                int
		want               string
	}{
		{"Bob", "bob@example.com", "555-1234", 25, "Customer: Bob (age: 25, email: bob@example.com, phone: 555-1234)"},
		{"", "bob@example.com", "555-1234", 25, "Error: name is required"},
		{"Bob", "bob@example.com", "555-1234", -1, "Error: age must be positive"},
		{"Bob", "", "555-1234", 25, "Error: email is required"},
		{"Bob", "bob@example.com", "", 25, "Error: phone is required"},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := ProcessCustomer(tt.name, tt.age, tt.email, tt.phone)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("ProcessCustomer(%q, %d, %q, %q) = %q, want %q", tt.name, tt.age, tt.email, tt.phone, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}

func TestProcessInvoice(t *testing.T) {
	tests := []struct {
		id, status string
		amount     float64
		want       string
	}{
		{"INV-001", "paid", 150.00, "Invoice: INV-001 (amount: 150.00, status: paid)"},
		{"", "paid", 150.00, "Error: id is required"},
		{"INV-001", "paid", -1, "Error: amount must be positive"},
		{"INV-001", "", 150.00, "Error: status is required"},
	}
	for _, tt := range tests {
		got := ProcessInvoice(tt.id, tt.amount, tt.status)
		if got != tt.want {
			t.Errorf("ProcessInvoice(%q, %f, %q) = %q, want %q", tt.id, tt.amount, tt.status, got, tt.want)
		}
	}
}
GOEOF

import struct

# Read encrypted WAL
enc = open('main.db-wal.encrypted', 'rb').read()

# Test XOR decryption
xored = bytes([b ^ 0x42 for b in enc])

# WAL header: each field is big-endian 32-bit
magic, ver, pgsz, chkpt, salt1, salt2, cksum1, cksum2 = struct.unpack('>8I', xored[:32])
print(f'Magic:   0x{magic:08x}')
print(f'Version: {ver} (0x{ver:08x}) expected ~3001000')
print(f'Page sz: {pgsz}')
print(f'Chkpt:   {chkpt}')
print(f'Salt1:   0x{salt1:08x}')
print(f'Salt2:   0x{salt2:08x}')

# Also try different XOR values just to explore
# The magic 377f0682 is the target for bytes 0-3
target_magic = struct.unpack('>I', bytes([0x37, 0x7f, 0x06, 0x82]))[0]
enc_magic_bytes = enc[0:4]

    page_data = dec[offset+24:offset+24+page_size]

    print(f'Freeblock: {first_freeblock}, Cells: {num_cells}, Content start: {cell_content_start}')
    
    # Read cell pointers (each 2 bytes, big-endian)
    cell_pointers = []

        ptr = struct.unpack('>H', page_data[8 + i*2 : 10 + i*2])[0]
        cell_pointers.append(ptr)

    for i, ptr in enumerate(cell_pointers):

        elif val < 0xC0:

            varint_size = 2

        else:
            payload_len = val & 0x7f
            varint_size = 1
            while page_data[ptr+varint_size-1] & 0x80:

        # Simplified: read varint
        payload_len = 0

        while True:
            b = page_data[ptr + vi]

            vi += 1

        # Parse values based on serial types

                values.append(None)
            elif st == 1:  # 8-bit signed integer

            elif st == 6:  # 64-bit signed integer
                values.append(struct.unpack('>q', payload[vi:vi+8])[0])
                vi += 8
            elif st == 7:  # 64-bit float
                values.append(struct.unpack('>d', payload[vi:vi+8])[0])

            elif st >= 12 and st % 2 == 0:  # BLOB: (st-12)/2 bytes

            elif st >= 13 and st % 2 == 1:  # TEXT: (st-13)/2 bytes
                text_len = (st - 13) // 2

import struct

def read_varint(data, offset):
    """Read a SQLite varint from data at offset, return (value, bytes_consumed)"""
    result = 0
    for i in range(9):
        b = data[offset + i]
        result = (result << 7) | (b & 0x7f)
        if not (b & 0x80):
            return result, i + 1
    return result, 9

def parse_cell_data(payload):
    """Parse the data in a SQLite cell given the payload (after payload_length and rowid varints)"""
    # First varint in payload is the header size
    header_size, consumed = read_varint(payload, 0)
    
    # Parse serial types (each is a varint)
    serial_types = []
    pos = consumed
    while pos < header_size:
        st, consumed = read_varint(payload, pos)
        serial_types.append(st)
        pos += consumed
    
    # Parse values
    values = []
    pos = header_size
    for st in serial_types:
        if st == 0:  # NULL
            values.append(None)
        elif st == 1:  # 8-bit signed int
            v = payload[pos]
            if v >= 128:
                v -= 256
            values.append(v)
            pos += 1
        elif st == 2:  # 16-bit signed int
            values.append(struct.unpack('>h', payload[pos:pos+2])[0])
            pos += 2
        elif st == 3:  # 24-bit signed int
            v = payload[pos] << 16 | payload[pos+1] << 8 | payload[pos+2]
            if v & 0x800000:
                v -= 0x1000000
            values.append(v)
            pos += 3
        elif st == 4:  # 32-bit signed int
            values.append(struct.unpack('>i', payload[pos:pos+4])[0])
            pos += 4
        elif st == 5:  # 48-bit signed int
            v = 0
            for j in range(6):
                v = (v << 8) | payload[pos+j]
            if v & 0x800000000000:
                v -= 0x1000000000000
            values.append(v)
            pos += 6
        elif st == 6:  # 64-bit signed int
            values.append(struct.unpack('>q', payload[pos:pos+8])[0])
            pos += 8
        elif st == 7:  # 64-bit float
            values.append(struct.unpack('>d', payload[pos:pos+8])[0])
            pos += 8
        elif st == 8:  # 0 (zero)
            values.append(0)
        elif st == 9:  # 1 (one)
            values.append(1)
        elif st == 10 or st == 11:
            values.append(None)
        elif st >= 12 and st % 2 == 0:  # BLOB: (st-12)/2 bytes
            blob_len = (st - 12) // 2
            values.append(payload[pos:pos+blob_len])
            pos += blob_len
        elif st >= 13 and st % 2 == 1:  # TEXT: (st-13)/2 bytes
            text_len = (st - 13) // 2
            values.append(payload[pos:pos+text_len].decode('utf-8', errors='replace'))
            pos += text_len
    
    return serial_types, values

    page_data = dec[offset+24:offset+24+page_size]
    
    print(f'\n=== Frame {fn}: pgno={pgno}, dbsize={dbsize} ===')

    first_freeblock = struct.unpack('>H', page_data[1:3])[0]

    # Read cell pointers (each 2 bytes, starting at offset 8)
    cell_pointers = []
    for i in range(num_cells):
        ptr = struct.unpack('>H', page_data[8 + i*2 : 10 + i*2])[0]
        cell_pointers.append(ptr)

with open('primers.fasta') as f:
    primer_content = f.read()
primers_raw = primer_content.strip().split('>')[1:]
primers = {}
for p in primers_raw:
    lines = p.strip().split('\n')
    header = lines[0]
    seq = ''.join(lines[1:]).upper()
    primers[header] = seq

print('Primers loaded:')
for h, s in primers.items():
    print(f'  {h}: {len(s)}bp')

# The forward primer ends with GAG (last 3 bases of left flank)
fwd = primers['forward_tm62.0']
rev = primers['reverse_tm63.3']

def revcomp(seq):
    comp = {'A': 'T', 'T': 'A', 'C': 'G', 'G': 'C'}
    return ''.join(comp.get(b, b) for b in reversed(seq))

# Verify forward primer
insertion = 'TAGATTAGAAGAAGAATTAAGAAGAAGATTAACAGAAAG'
rc_insertion = revcomp(insertion)

print()
print('=== Forward Primer Verification ===')
assert fwd.startswith(insertion), f'Forward does not start with insertion'
print(f'✓ Starts with insertion ({len(insertion)}bp)')

# 3' annealing part should match the end of left flank
anneal_fwd = fwd[len(insertion):]
assert len(anneal_fwd) >= 15, f'Forward annealing too short: {len(anneal_fwd)}'
assert len(anneal_fwd) <= 45, f'Forward annealing too long: {len(anneal_fwd)}'
print(f'✓ Annealing length: {len(anneal_fwd)}bp (15-45 OK)')

assert anneal_fwd == inp[215-len(anneal_fwd):215], f'Forward annealing does not match left flank'
print(f'✓ Annealing matches template left flank (pos {215-len(anneal_fwd)}-214)')

import subprocess
def calc_tm(seq):
    if len(seq) < 2 or len(seq) > 36:
        return -1
    result = subprocess.run(
        ['/opt/homebrew/bin/oligotm', '-tp', '1', '-sc', '1', '-mv', '50', '-dv', '2', '-n', '0.8', '-d', '500', seq],
        capture_output=True, text=True, timeout=10
    )
    if result.returncode == 0:
        return float(result.stdout.strip())
    return -1

fwd_tm = calc_tm(anneal_fwd)
print(f'✓ Forward Tm: {fwd_tm:.1f}°C (58-72 OK)')

print()
print('=== Reverse Primer Verification ===')
assert rev.startswith(rc_insertion), f'Reverse does not start with rc_insertion'
print(f'✓ Starts with rc_insertion ({len(rc_insertion)}bp)')

anneal_rev = rev[len(rc_insertion):]
assert len(anneal_rev) >= 15, f'Reverse annealing too short: {len(anneal_rev)}'
assert len(anneal_rev) <= 45, f'Reverse annealing too long: {len(anneal_rev)}'
print(f'✓ Annealing length: {len(anneal_rev)}bp (15-45 OK)')

# Reverse primer annealing is rev comp of right flank start

assert anneal_rev == expected_anneal, f'Reverse annealing mismatch'
print(f'✓ Annealing matches rev comp of right flank (pos 215-{215+len(anneal_rev)-1})')

rev_tm = calc_tm(anneal_rev)
print(f'✓ Reverse Tm: {rev_tm:.1f}°C (58-72 OK)')

print()
print('=== Pair Verification ===')
tm_diff = abs(fwd_tm - rev_tm)
print(f'✓ Tm difference: {tm_diff:.1f}°C (<=5 OK)')
assert tm_diff <= 5.0, f'Tm difference too large: {tm_diff}'

# Verify the primers would produce the correct output
# The forward primer's insertion + the reverse primer's rc_insertion should be 
# complementary (for circularization)
print(f'✓ Forward and reverse ends are complementary: {revcomp(insertion) == rc_insertion}')

# Final check: after PCR and circularization, we should get the output
# For this to work, the 3' ends of both primers should be back-to-back on the template
# Forward 3' end: position 214 (last base of left flank)
# Reverse 3' end: position 215 (first base of right flank)
print(f'✓ Primers are back-to-back (3'' ends at positions 214 and 215)')

print()
print('=== Design Summary ===')
print(f'Forward: {len(fwd)}bp, annealing Tm = {fwd_tm:.1f}°C')
print(f'Reverse: {len(rev)}bp, annealing Tm = {rev_tm:.1f}°C')
print(f'Tm difference: {tm_diff:.1f}°C')
print(f'Number of primer pairs: 1 (minimum required)')
print()
print('ALL CHECKS PASSED ✓')

from feal import *
import random

# Understand the cipher structure by simulating with known keys
# Let me trace through the encryption step by step

def trace_encrypt(plaintext):
    left = getleft(plaintext)
    right = getright(plaintext)
    
    print(f'Input: left={hex(left)}, right={hex(right)}')
    
    left ^= key[0]
    right ^= key[1]
    print(f'After whitening: left={hex(left)}, right={hex(right)}')
    
    right ^= left
    print(f'After pre-mix: left={hex(left)}, right={hex(right)}')
    
    for round_num in range(4):
        mixed = f_function(right ^ key[round_num + 2])
        print(f'  Round {round_num}: right={hex(right)}, key={hex(key[round_num+2])}, right^key={hex(right ^ key[round_num+2])}, mixed={hex(mixed)}')
        temp = right
        right = left ^ mixed
        left = temp
        print(f'  Round {round_num} result: left={hex(left)}, right={hex(right)}')
    
    left ^= right
    print(f'After post-mix: left={hex(left)}, right={hex(right)}')
    
    result = merge(right, left)
    print(f'Output: {hex(result)}')
    return result

# Set up known keys

create_random_keys()
print('Keys:')
for i in range(6):
    print(f'  key[{i}] = {hex(key[i])}')

from feal import *

# Check for differential probabilities in f_function
# Look for values where f(a) == f(a ^ delta) for various deltas
import random

# Check specific high-probability differentials
def diff_prob(delta, num_samples=10000):
    count = 0
    for _ in range(num_samples):
        a = random.randint(0, 0xFFFFFFFF)
        if f_function(a) ^ f_function(a ^ delta) == 0:
            count += 1
    return count / num_samples

# Try various delta values  
for delta in [0x80000000, 0x00800000, 0x00008000, 0x00000080, 0x80808080, 0x01010101, 0x80008000]:
    prob = diff_prob(delta, 5000)
    print(f'Delta {hex(delta)}: zero-output-diff probability = {prob:.4f}')

# Look for the most common output differential
def most_common_diff(delta, num_samples=50000):
    from collections import Counter
    diffs = Counter()
    for _ in range(num_samples):
        a = random.randint(0, 0xFFFFFFFF)
        d = f_function(a) ^ f_function(a ^ delta)
        diffs[d] += 1
    return diffs.most_common(5)

for delta in [0x80800000, 0x80000000, 0x00800000, 0x00000080]:
    print(f'Delta {hex(delta)}: most common diffs:')
    for d, cnt in most_common_diff(delta, 20000):
        print(f'  {hex(d)}: {cnt} ({cnt/20000*100:.1f}%)')
"

from feal import *
from collections import Counter

# Analyze differential properties more systematically

# Check: for delta 0x80800000, output diff is ALWAYS 0x02000000
def check_always(delta, output_delta, trials=10000):
    import random
    for _ in range(trials):
        a = random.randint(0, 0xFFFFFFFF)
        if f_function(a) ^ f_function(a ^ delta) != output_delta:
            return False, a
    return True, None

print('Delta 0x80800000 -> output 0x02000000 (always?):')

        line = line.strip()

current_section = []

    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        if line == 'M486 S0':
            in_text = True
            continue
        elif line == 'M486 S-1' and in_text:
            in_text = False
            if current_section:
                text_sections.append(current_section)
                current_section = []
            continue

            if line.startswith('G1 ') or line.startswith('G0 '):
                current_section.append(line)

# Analyze X,Y,E coordinates from all sections
for i, section in enumerate(text_sections):
    extrusion_moves = []
    for line in section:
        x = re.search(r'X([0-9.]+)', line)
        y = re.search(r'Y([0-9.]+)', line)
        e = re.search(r'E(-?[0-9.]+)', line)
        if x and y:
            xv = float(x.group(1))
            yv = float(y.group(1))
            ev = float(e.group(1)) if e else 0
            if ev > 0:  # Only actual extrusion moves
                extrusion_moves.append((xv, yv, ev))
    
    x_vals = [m[0] for m in extrusion_moves]
    y_vals = [m[1] for m in extrusion_moves]

        print(f"  Y range: {min(y_vals):.3f} - {max(y_vals):.3f}")
        print(f"  Extrusion count: {len(x_vals)}")
        
        # Try to identify letter groups by looking for X clusters
        sorted_x = sorted(set(x_vals))
        if len(sorted_x) > 10:
            gaps = [sorted_x[j+1] - sorted_x[j] for j in range(len(sorted_x)-1)]
            big_gaps = [(sorted_x[j], sorted_x[j+1], g) for j, g in enumerate(gaps) if g > 0.5]
            if big_gaps:
                print(f"  X clusters (gaps > 0.5):")
                for g in big_gaps:
                    print(f"    Gap at X={g[0]:.3f} to {g[1]:.3f} (gap={g[2]:.3f})")
        
        print()

import re

# Parse more carefully - track by M486 S0 for text and M486 S1 for box
text_sections = {}
box_sections = {}
current_type = None
current_lines = []
current_snum = None

with open('text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        
        if line.startswith('M486 S') and not line.startswith('M486 SA'):
            snum_match = re.search(r'M486 S(-?\d+)', line)
            if snum_match:
                sn = int(snum_match.group(1))
                if sn == 0 or sn == 1:
                    # End previous section
                    if current_type is not None and current_lines:
                        if current_type == 'text':
                            text_sections.setdefault(current_snum, []).append(current_lines)
                        elif current_type == 'box':
                            box_sections.setdefault(current_snum, []).append(current_lines)
                    
                    current_type = 'text' if sn == 0 else 'box'
                    current_lines = []
                    current_snum = len(text_sections.get('text_key', [])) if current_type == 'text' else len(box_sections.get('box_key', []))
                elif sn == -1:
                    # End of section
                    if current_type is not None and current_lines:
                        if current_type == 'text':
                            text_sections.setdefault(current_snum, []).append(current_lines)
                        elif current_type == 'box':
                            box_sections.setdefault(current_snum, []).append(current_lines)
                    current_type = None
                    current_lines = []
                    current_snum = None
                continue
        
        if current_type and (line.startswith('G1 ') or line.startswith('G0 ')):
            current_lines.append(line)

print("Text sections:", len(text_sections))
print("Box sections:", len(box_sections))

# Let me redo this more simply
text_layers = []
box_layers = []
current_obj = None
current_layer = []

with open('text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        
        if line.startswith('M486 S'):
            snum_match = re.search(r'M486 S(-?\d+)', line)
            if snum_match:
                sn = int(snum_match.group(1))
                if sn == 0:
                    # End previous
                    if current_obj == 'box' and current_layer:
                        box_layers.append(current_layer)
                    current_obj = 'text'
                    current_layer = []
                elif sn == 1:
                    # End previous
                    if current_obj == 'text' and current_layer:
                        text_layers.append(current_layer)
                    current_obj = 'box'
                    current_layer = []
                elif sn == -1:
                    if current_obj == 'text' and current_layer:
                        text_layers.append(current_layer)
                    elif current_obj == 'box' and current_layer:
                        box_layers.append(current_layer)
                    current_obj = None
                    current_layer = []
                continue
        
        if current_obj and (line.startswith('G1 ') or line.startswith('G0 ')):
            current_layer.append(line)

print(f"\nText layers: {len(text_layers)}")
print(f"Box layers: {len(box_layers)}")

for i, layer in enumerate(text_layers):

import re

# Parse text layers
text_layers = []
current_obj = None
current_layer = []

with open('text.gcode') as f:
    for line in f:
        line = line.strip()
        
        if line.startswith('M486 S'):
            snum_match = re.search(r'M486 S(-?\d+)', line)
            if snum_match:
                sn = int(snum_match.group(1))
                if sn == 0:
                    if current_obj == 'box' and current_layer:
                        pass  # ignore box
                    current_obj = 'text'
                    current_layer = []
                elif sn == 1:
                    if current_obj == 'text' and current_layer:
                        text_layers.append(current_layer)
                    current_obj = 'box'
                    current_layer = []
                elif sn == -1:
                    if current_obj == 'text' and current_layer:
                        text_layers.append(current_layer)
                    elif current_obj == 'box' and current_layer:
                        pass  # ignore box
                    current_obj = None
                    current_layer = []
                continue
        
        if current_obj == 'text' and (line.startswith('G1 ') or line.startswith('G0 ')):
            current_layer.append(line)

print(f"Total text layers: {len(text_layers)}")

# Analyze layer 5 (mid-layer with decent data)
layer = text_layers[5]
print(f"Layer 5: {len(layer)} lines")

# Extract all moves with coordinates
moves = []
for line in layer:
    xm = re.search(r'X([0-9.]+)', line)
    ym = re.search(r'Y([0-9.]+)', line)
    em = re.search(r'E(-?[0-9.]+)', line)
    xv = float(xm.group(1)) if xm else None
    yv = float(ym.group(1)) if ym else None
    ev = float(em.group(1)) if em else 0.0
    moves.append((xv, yv, ev))

# Get first few moves to understand path tracing
print("\nFirst 20 moves of layer 5:")
for i, (x, y, e) in enumerate(moves[:20]):
    print(f"  {i}: X={x}, Y={y}, E={e:.5f}")

# Get min/max
x_vals = [m[0] for m in moves if m[0] is not None]
y_vals = [m[1] for m in moves if m[1] is not None]
print(f"\nX range: {min(x_vals):.3f} to {max(x_vals):.3f}")
print(f"Y range: {min(y_vals):.3f} to {max(y_vals):.3f}")

# Let me also look at what happens with Z values in this layer
z_vals = []
for line in layer:
    zm = re.search(r'Z([0-9.]+)', line)
    if zm:
        z_vals.append(float(zm.group(1)))
if z_vals:

    ym = re.search(r'Y([0-9.]+)', line)
    em = re.search(r'E(-?[0-9.]+)', line)
    
    if xm: cur_x = float(xm.group(1))
    if ym: cur_y = float(ym.group(1))
    ev = float(em.group(1)) if em else 0.0
    
    if ev > 0 and cur_x is not None and cur_y is not None:
        ext_coords.append((cur_x, cur_y))

# Create a better visualization by carefully handling the diagonal nature
# Scale the coordinates

max_x = max(c[0] for c in ext_coords)
min_y = min(c[1] for c in ext_coords)
max_y = max(c[1] for c in ext_coords)

# Use a projection that rotates the coordinates to make the text horizontal
# First, find the angle of the text by linear regression
import math
xs = [c[0] for c in ext_coords]
ys = [c[1] for c in ext_coords]

n = len(xs)
sum_x = sum(xs)
sum_y = sum(ys)
sum_xy = sum(x*y for x,y in ext_coords)
sum_x2 = sum(x*x for x in xs)

# Slope of the text direction
if n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x != 0:
    slope = (n * sum_xy - sum_x * sum_y) / (n * sum_x2 - sum_x * sum_x)
    angle = math.atan(slope)
    print(f"Text slope: {slope:.4f}, angle: {math.degrees(angle):.2f} degrees")
    
    # Rotate all points by -angle to make the text horizontal
    cos_a = math.cos(-angle)
    sin_a = math.sin(-angle)
    
    rotated_coords = []
    for x, y in ext_coords:
        rx = x * cos_a - y * sin_a
        ry = x * sin_a + y * cos_a
        rotated_coords.append((rx, ry))
    
    # Get rotated bounds
    r_min_x = min(c[0] for c in rotated_coords)
    r_max_x = max(c[0] for c in rotated_coords)
    r_min_y = min(c[1] for c in rotated_coords)
    r_max_y = max(c[1] for c in rotated_coords)
    
    print(f"Rotated X: {r_min_x:.3f} to {r_max_x:.3f} (width: {r_max_x-r_min_x:.3f})")
    print(f"Rotated Y: {r_min_y:.3f} to {r_max_y:.3f} (height: {r_max_y-r_min_y:.3f})")
    
    # Create grid visualization with rotated coords
    w = 120
    h = 40
    grid = [[' ' for _ in range(w)] for _ in range(h)]
    
    r_x_range = r_max_x - r_min_x
    r_y_range = r_max_y - r_min_y
    
    for rx, ry in rotated_coords:
        gx = int((rx - r_min_x) / r_x_range * (w - 1))
        gy = int((ry - r_min_y) / r_y_range * (h - 1))
        gy = h - 1 - gy
        if 0 <= gx < w and 0 <= gy < h:
            grid[gy][gx] = '#'
    
    print(f"\nRotated visualization ({w}x{h}):")
    for row in grid:
        line = ''.join(row)
        if line.strip():
            print(line)

# Extract paths (sequences of extrusion moves)
paths = []
current_path = []
cur_x = None
cur_y = None
in_extrusion = False

    ym = re.search(r'Y([0-9.]+)', line)
    em = re.search(r'E(-?[0-9.]+)', line)
    
    if xm: cur_x = float(xm.group(1))
    if ym: cur_y = float(ym.group(1))
    ev = float(em.group(1)) if em else 0.0
    
    if ev > 0 and cur_x is not None and cur_y is not None:
        current_path.append((cur_x, cur_y, ev))
        in_extrusion = True
    elif ev < 0 and current_path:
        # Retraction
        paths.append(current_path)
        current_path = []
        in_extrusion = False
    elif ev == 0 and 'E-' not in line:
        if in_extrusion and current_path:

            pass  # Keep collecting since moves without E might still be in same path

# Add last path if any
if current_path:

# Simpler approach: extract ALL extrusion points from layer 9

cur_y = None

    if xm: cur_x = float(xm.group(1))
    if ym: cur_y = float(ym.group(1))
    ev = float(em.group(1)) if em else 0.0
    
    if ev > 0 and cur_x is not None and cur_y is not None:

min_x = min(p[0] for p in ext_points)
max_x = max(p[0] for p in ext_points)
min_y = min(p[1] for p in ext_points)
max_y = max(p[1] for p in ext_points)

print(f"X: {min_x:.3f} to {max_x:.3f} (width: {max_x-min_x:.3f})")
print(f"Y: {min_y:.3f} to {max_y:.3f} (height: {max_y-min_y:.3f})")

# Create high-res visualization
w = 80
h = 60
grid = [[' ' for _ in range(w)] for _ in range(h)]

x_range = max_x - min_x
y_range = max_y - min_y

for x, y in ext_points:
    gx = int((x - min_x) / x_range * (w - 1)) if x_range > 0 else 0
    gy = int((y - min_y) / y_range * (h - 1)) if y_range > 0 else 0
    gy = h - 1 - gy
    if 0 <= gx < w and 0 <= gy < h:
